summary:
The presentation of proptosis and painful orbitopathy developing within two days of head/eye trauma suggests an acute vascular event such as a carotid-cavernous fistula due to traumatic disruption of vessels leading into cavernous sinus. This differential outweighs other causes including fracture because it better fits both symptom onset timeline and associated findings without requiring structural bone abnormalities seen clinically.
